title: Page-level content: admin editor UX (tabs optional)
description: Admin-side UX for page body content: a newly created page opens straight into an editable body with no 'create a tab first' step, and tabs become an optional way to add extra named views. Builds on the data model (TASK-65) and pipeline (TASK-66).
acceptanceCriteria: Creating a page and clicking it opens an editable body immediately — no need to create a tab first
When a page has no user-defined tabs, no tab bar is shown; the editor edits the page body directly
Adding one or more tabs shows them as additional named views; the primary/body container is never shown as a removable/renamable user tab
Publish and history controls stay at page level (as they already are in `TabContentPage.tsx`) and act on body + tabs together
The editor route(s) resolve correctly: reaching a page shows its body, and `/pages/:pageId/tabs/:tabId` still works for user tabs

steps.description: Routing + entry to the body editor
steps.details: In `apps/admin`, make clicking a page in `SidebarTree` land on its body editor (decide the route: reuse `/pages/:pageId` for the body, keeping `/pages/:pageId/tabs/:tabId` for user tabs). Mount `ContentEditor` on the primary container's blocks.

steps.description: Hide the primary container from the tab UI
steps.details: Update `SidebarTree` and `TabContentPage` so the primary/body container is not listed as a user tab (no rename/delete/reorder on it). The '+ Nova tab' affordance still creates optional additional tabs.

Show a tab bar only when the page has ≥1 user tab; otherwise the page is just its body. When tabs exist, present body + tabs coherently (decide whether the body is the first view or a distinct 'Overview').

Create a page, type body content without adding a tab, confirm autosave + publish work; then add a tab, confirm both are editable and the tab bar appears; confirm the primary container can't be deleted/renamed as a tab.

technicalNotes: `ContentEditor` mounts per container with `key=` and loads `getByTab` with `gcTime: 0` — keep that pattern when the body becomes its own editor instance.
Publish already flushes the active editor before snapshotting (`TabContentPage.handlePublish`); ensure the body editor participates in that flush.
